reviews.review_title: One of the Best Free VR Demos Out There
reviews.review_description:
I honestly don’t get the hate some people are giving this game. This is a genuinely impressive demo — and yes, it’s completely free. The visuals alone blew me away. For something that isn’t even a full release, the graphics are sharp, immersive, and surprisingly polished. You get detailed cockpit interiors, solid lighting effects, and a great sense of scale and motion — all on standalone VR.

Now, let’s talk about the controls. They’re smooth and intuitive, which is crucial for any flight experience. I did notice some issues when first trying out the career mode — mainly with responsiveness or certain inputs not registering — but it’s clear the devs listened and patched it fast. Fixing bugs in a demo version? That says a lot about how seriously they’re taking this.

Of course, it’s not perfect. The demo lacks key features like active combat (you can’t fire weapons yet), dynamic weather or time-of-day settings, and more variety in the mission structure. But let’s be real: this is a demo, not a AAA flight sim like Microsoft Flight Simulator. And still, it offers enough gameplay and world-building to keep you interested.

If the full version expands on what’s already here — adds deeper mechanics, fleshes out the missions, and introduces more interactivity — I’d absolutely pay $30–50 without hesitation. The foundation is rock-solid. This demo shows real potential, and I’m hyped to see where it goes.

To the devs: mad respect. Keep pushing forward. You’ve got something special here.

developer_response.body:
Hi Xavstone,

Thanks for taking the time to write this review. Feedback of this quality is extremely helpful!

Regarding the controls — the latest update (0.9.40) has addressed a major issue relating to the control of the aircraft. Maybe give it another shot.

Regarding combat — https://www.patreon.com/posts/wheres-combat-134613847
Join our Patreon as a free member and read this article. It explains why there's no combat in Formation!

Thanks again for your review!

reviews.review_description: The only thing im sad about is no carrier takeoff, honest

reviews.review_title: Has the potential to be a great game
reviews.review_description:
I would guess many of the lower ratings are from people who quit before getting through the initial tutorial. The controls are a bit hard to master, and admittedly a bit frustrating at first, but they get easier as you practice.

Once you get the controls down, the game actually becomes pretty chill. I could see playing this to de-stress after a long day.

That being said, the controls do present a bit of a learning curve. The game is in “Easy” mode for Early Access, but it feels more like a “Hard” mode. I’d be curious to try a version where the vertical axis input is disabled for aileron rolls, and the horizontal axis is disabled for full loops.

The music and sound effects are great. They create a sci-fi ambiance that feels something like being immersed in Juno Reactor’s “Rotorblade”. Listening to the music may be my favorite part of the game.

Looking forward to playing the full version!

developer_response.body:
Hi AppleFlowerMonster! 

Thanks for taking the time to write such a detailed review. The most recent update (0.9.40) has addressed a major issue relating to the controls that you mentioned. There should now be a more distinct separation of X and Y axes making executing maneuvers much easier (and less frustrating).  Thanks for your feedback on this - reviews like yours go a long way in aiding production!

Glad you like the music too!

reviews.review_description: Time consuming

As soon as I loaded up the game I was impressed by even the loading screen, it was awesome and it got me really excited to try out the game for real. I haven’t even gotten past the third mission yet but i find the whole futuristic concept awesome and even the little tidbits of lore are great. The game is genuinely difficult but it makes me want to keep coming back to play it more. I love games like that, and I’m no pilot but I love flight sims. This may not be an ultra realistic one but it’s a lot better than most you can get on the meta store. I’ve only encountered one small problem, and that’s whenever I try to push the speed bar/handle forward it either doesnt register me grabbing it or when the grabbing finally works it goes forward for a few seconds then goes full brakes for some reason. It’s pretty annoying but it hasn’t really affected my formation in the mission so I just wanted to let you guys know. Thanks for making such a great game!

developer_response.body:
Firstly, thanks for your review! Some very encouraging words.

Regarding the throttle issue; the instant full brake issue occurs when the controller momentarily loses tracking. Try to make sure you're in a well lit environment and not moving your controllers out of the view of the headset. We might be able to fix this so thanks for reporting it.

Cheers!

reviews.review_description: This is hard but very relaxing and interesting for non-gamers like me. I can't get enough.
Way too much handle control needed

Appleflowermonster was right im loving the game but sometimes when i want to do a loop i get to high and then i die so if one of the developers 
See this please increase the hight of the altitude thank you

developer_response.body:
Flight ceiling is ~60000 ft before engines fail (similar to an F15).  Keep an eye on your altitude! And maybe don't try to do a loop at mach 2 :) Maximum dynamic response is ~420 KTs so stick to that speed if you want to do loops. But judging by your handle this might be too much to ask!

Thanks for the 5 stars!

Hey devs just wanna say I love the game but I have one problem with the demo I can't get past step 5 the continue button just doesn't appear which is shame because this game looks so fun

developer_response.body:
Hi, super glad you got it working in the end and thanks for taking the time to leave a review.

To anyone reading this with the same issue: press and hold the GRIP TRIGGER (not the index finger trigger) then move the controller in any direction.

Update: Regarding the review title — THANK YOU!

Update 2: We added a 3D touch controller model that points to the grip trigger. This should prevent any confusion in future.

reviews.review_title: Game not ready for prime time!
reviews.review_description:
I was fighter pilot and the whole diamond in hud was ridiculous as it does not work.  I can pass the diamond in the hud part, but only after several tries.  There is too much guessing on when to move the diamond- mostly too soon or too late.  Once get to actually try the roll, within 2 seconds ot says fail with no explanation as to why.  Sorry, but after 30 minutes, I stopped and deleted game.

**** In reply to developer question:  No option to reply to your answer but I flew FA-18s.

developer_response.body:
Hi BeavH82. We're sorry you're having issues with the timing. There's 3 red countdown bars, a 3,2,1 counter, audio cues, a target position guide to follow, and an extensive tutorial with markers showing when to start your maneuver. Respectfully, there's very little guess work involved. Most people don't seem to have an issue with the timing and really enjoy the system once they get the timing down. We've also fixed a bug which affected the flight model. Maybe give it another shot?

And wow... what did you fly?

**** thanks for replying. Now that we've got you, any chance you could join our other testers in our full version test build? And have you tried the new build? We rarely encounter this issue and would appreciate you providing feedback. Send your email to support@infamusgames.com and we'll send you an invite - all info on how to do this is included.

Then it brings you back to the plane for a second but even when you put the diamond in the diamond it still says you’re out of formation. And it just keeps going over and over and there’s no way to just fly around and feel it out. Offer a free fly mode so people can feel it out and figure out what to do before they go into the training

developer_response.body:
We recently updated the tutorial to make it much less difficult. There was a major issue where having the trigger enabled before the MARK would basically fail the test every time.   There was also a bug with the handling of the aircraft. This is now fixed too. Please give it another shot!

Also, any chance you could join our other testers in our full version test build? We haven't encountered this issue and would appreciate you providing video. Send your email to support@infamusgames.com and we'll send you an invite - all info on how to do this is included.

PS There's also a free fly mode in the main menu.

The core game and the idea of it is really good and the free play is extremely fun. It’s just when you come todo the campaign it’s very frustrating. I do feel that adding some extra instructions to this level to help new players would be amazing. As it’s the 2nd level you can’t expect players to have figured this out already.

reviews.review_description: The game looks great and I can’t wait to play the full thing HOWEVER the margin of error is so little that even on the easiest possible difficulty, it took almost 20 attempts at the second level and I still have yet to complete the third level without falling out of formation at least once. No matter how accurate I try to be, whenever it wants me to pull up, I almost always fall out of formation. Like I said though, the game is great and what is there looks great, I especially love how you can watch the replay. It’s just either the margin of error needs to be increased or the aircraft’s sensitivity to input needs to be decreased.

developer_response.body:
Hi, the margin of error is pretty small but once you master the timing the difficulty drops off pretty quickly. We are aiming to improve the controls and tutorial to make it a little easier. 20 attempts is actually impressive for a beginner! Hopefully it felt rewarding completing it. 

Thanks for your review and support!

Update: Maybe give the newest version a shot! We have made some changes that will hopefully improve the experience.

reviews.review_description: This game is well worth the free demo! The graphics are good! The flying experience as a whole felt awesome! I can’t wait for the game to come out and will definitely be buying. Formation flying is challenging but I felt it was a realistic experience!

The game honestly sucked from the very beginning. I wish I was able to play this game long enough to get out of the tutorial but unfortunately I don’t have 45 minutes to spare on a TUTORIAL LEVEL. The tutorial expects you to have reflexes that move at the speed of sound. If the diamond thingy in the tutorial move a nanometer to the right and you don’t follow it at the exact same time it will restart you from the beginning of the tutorial. It’s a shame I didn’t get to play it. The game looked fun.

developer_response.body:
Hi, thanks for your review. 

This game depends on the player having timing about as accurate as rhythm games like Beat Saber for example. That being said, we are still aiming to make the tutorial better so maybe give it another shot after the next update.  

Finally and with respect, you don't have to restart the tutorial from the beginning after failing a trial. We took pains to make sure you can very quickly retry each maneuver in the tutorial precisely to avoid frustration and wasting the player's time. 

Saying that you have to restart the tutorial from the beginning every time is simply a false statement. If you can prove otherwise please respond here or via our support email (support@infamusgames.com). 

Thanks again for your feedback!

reviews.review_description: I love the idea and the graphics looks good and it feels immersive. But please make the flight dynamics and controls more realistic.

reviews.review_title: Low ratings are bullsh*t—the game rules!
reviews.review_description:
4 months ago I went to a Blue Angels show where the best of the best of the Navy’s aviators performed in perfect unison, this game gave me the same thrill and awe that watching those pilots gave me.

The movements you need to make, even on the easiest difficulty, have a margin of error so low that I thought that I was experiencing a glitch for my first hour or so of banging my head against a wall, but after you get the hang of it, it really clicks and you’re met with a spectacular game.

The style of it is also unmistakably cool, with the futuristic architecture and modern day planes being paired with an 80s style of graphical displays making a spectacular effect.

By far my favorite part of the game has to be the replays. After ages of trying to complete a level and failing again and again, being able to view your successful attempt with the single best plane audio I’ve ever witnessed is simply mind numbing.

My one issue is that simply letting go of the joystick or throttle resets it to its default position, this makes finishing maneuvers extremely easy simply by letting go. This resulted in me being able to complete maneuvers that I otherwise wouldn’t have, and made me feel like I cheated the game into giving me a mission success. To preserve the difficulty, I’d recommend having the stick and throttle remain where it was when you let go.

I’m also not super familiar with what the plane that you pilot is. At first I thought that it was an F-18, but it looks more like a combo between an F-22 and Su-34, what exactly is the plane? Also: I’d love to be able to change the plane itself into different ones like F-22s, F-18s, or F-35s once the final game releases, but that’s not high on the priority list.

10/10 game even in early access, the sadness I felt upon completing the only 3 levels was heartbreaking. I hope that the devs do well, and I’m happy to pay for Formation once it releases!
Its a fun game and all but POSSIBLY, can first person be added? Not like super detailed but like perhaps a way to sit in the cockpit as your staying in formation?

developer_response.body:
Hi, Jipar. 

We have added a cockpit view which you can select by pressing the Left Thumbstick Button (it should also be the default view). 

Can you please tell us the version number you are running by quoting the number at the load screen on startup? Please write to support@infamusgames.com. Cheers!

reviews.review_description: Very cool game!!!
It’s a pretty good game but when i try to do flips i go under the formation and there’s not a way to easily go back up, this may just be a skill issue but it’s a good game either way

developer_response.body: Hi, thanks for your review! Can you give us more info? Where does this happen? Does it feel like you're fighting the controls to get back in position? Thanks

reviews.review_description: Now coming from VTOL VR I can appreciate what the devs are trying to do and it’s an understandable early access but the problem is the flight controls are very janky if you want to make a good flight sim the controls need to be smooth and responsive and the tutorial stage is unplayable because I get stuck on learning flight controls and can’t continue these are issues that should be touched on because they are core aspects of the game

Fulfill your fantasy of guiding a life like jet!
Top Gun!

This game is actually amazing, it's really fun and difficult and I like that. Challenge is what makes game fun. There are a few things thst do not like about this game though, the AI generated images of the people im not a fan of, and the main thing I do not like about this game is when your in the cockpit your body is just standing there and you can only move your arms with the plane controls, and it's sometimes really hard to aim the stick in the right way becouse its based on where your hand is positioned and your hand is invisible. A full body inverse kinematic (IK) would help greatly for immersion. But overall this is a really fun experience and I hope to see what the game turns into in the future!

developer_response.body:
Hi! 

Thanks for the detailed feedback. We will certainly take it all on board. We tried hard to make sure you know where your flightstick is but a free floating stick (i.e. the touch controller) was always going to pose a steep design challenge. You do get used to it but it takes a little time. A training mode/game (like in Helldivers 2 for the special weapons) might help fix this! Stay tuned.

Thanks again!
